title: Object Types, Interfaces, Aliases, and Structural Compatibility · Mastery Check
summary: Design public service records that evolve without silently accepting misspelled, mutable, or incompatible fields. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for an evolvable service-record contract library. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Model required, optional, readonly, indexed, and exact-optional property behavior under strict configuration.
Choose interface or type alias from extension, unions, declaration merging, diagnostics, and API intent.
Predict structural assignability while identifying where extra members, methods, and variance create risk.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.typescript:
// typescript-basics cumulative strict TypeScript portfolio.
// Add one typed behavior, runtime boundary, changed case, and evidence return per activity.

steps.title: Object Property Modifiers · changed case
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A scheduling record has id "job-5", an optional owner, readonly creation time, and a mutable status transition. Reject a misspelled status field, protect the invariant, and show which readonly promise exists only at compile time.

Which action demonstrates Object Property Modifiers without dropping earlier contracts?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for model required, optional, readonly, indexed, and exact-optional property behavior under strict configuration.
